Support Instructor (Deck & Engine) Job at Tetra Maritime




        
    

        
        Tetra Maritime is a leading Nigerian ship owner and operator, providing comprehensive and tailor-made solutions across the oil and gas supply chain, including upstream, midstream, and downstream.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Support Instructor (Deck & Engine)

Job Location: Lagos

Role Definition
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a dedicated and knowledgeable Maritime Instructor to join our faculty at Sealearn Academy.
	The successful candidate will be responsible for delivering high-quality instructional and vocational training in maritime subjects, preparing trainees for successful careers at sea and in related maritime industries.
	The instructor shall conduct theoretical and practical classes and ensure that training complies with various national and international standards such as STCW and IMO regulations.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and deliver vocational courses in nautical science, marine engineering, maritime safety, seamanship, cargo work, repair and maintenance.
	Fabricate props and mockups for delivery of practical training and skill development
	Conduct classroom instruction, practical training, and assessments.
	Provide skill enhancement support and career guidance to trainees and students.
	Maintain training records and ensure documentation is complete and compliant.
	Collaborate with instructors and staff to improve curriculum and student outcomes.
	Stay current with industry trends, maritime regulations, and educational best practices.
	Participate in meetings, professional development, and continuous improvement initiatives.
	Ensure classroom, workshop, practical workspace and laboratory have safety protocols in place and rigorously followed.


Requirements
Qualifications & Required Knowledge:


	Valid STCW certifications for Able Seafarer Deck / Engine and oil / chemical tanker endorsements.
	Minimum of 7 years of sea-going experience as a Bosun / Able Seafarer / Motorman/ Fitter (deck or engine) on board an ocean-going ship.
	Holds certificate of an Administration approved Train the Trainer course.
	Familiarity with IMO Model Course requirements and other industry standards
	Proficient in the use of machinery, equipment and tools and other maritime education technologies.


Core Competencies & Skills:


	 Teaching experience at a maritime academy or similar institution.
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
	Located near Apapa, Lagos.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Country Pharmacist",
  "description": "INTERSOS is an independent humanitarian organization that assists the victims of natural disasters, armed conflicts and exclusion. Its activities are based on the principles of solidarity, justice, human dignity, equality of rights and opportunities, respect for diversity and coexistence, paying special attention to the most vulnerable people.

We are&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Country Pharmacist

Code:&nbsp;SR-49-10159
Job Location: Maiduguri, Borno
Duty station:&nbsp;Maiduguri
Starting date: 01/04/2026 - emergency project
Contract duration:&nbsp;6 months (non-renewable)
Reporting to:&nbsp;Medical Coordinator
Supervision of:&nbsp;Pharmacy Manager
Type of Duty Station:&nbsp;Non-family duty station

General context of the project


	With over a decade of conflict, there have been devastating humanitarian consequences across North-East (Borno, Adamawa, and Yobe (BAY) states) and the North-West of Nigeria. According to the Humanitarian Needs Overview 2023, an estimated 8.3 million women, men, and children need some form of humanitarian and protection assistance.
	Over 2 million people have been forced to flee their homes due to persistent violence and remain internally displaced. Food insecurity and malnutrition are exacerbated by inflation, poverty, and restricted access due to insecurity, which impedes livelihood opportunities.
	Gender-based violence (GBV) has been a significant protection concern faced by IDP women and girls in the area.
	Numerous basic infrastructures, such as hospitals, clinics, and water supplies, are damaged, posing constraints on essential service provision. Epidemics like cholera arising from flooding and overcrowded settings put extra stress on the already vulnerable communities.


General purpose of the position


	Ensure proper mission pharmaceutical management and efficient functioning of INTERSOS pharmacies (procurement, storage, distribution, inventory, consumption and data management) at central and project level, ensuring adequate and timely supply of quality medical materials, equipment and drugs.
	In collaboration with logistics department monitor the pharmacy/medical warehouse storing activities in order to keep pharmacy stock above security level and guarantee the respect of required conditions (temperature, humidity, etc.).


Main responsibilities and tasks


	In collaboration with the logistics department and the Pharmacist Manager ensure storage, stock management and supply processes, as well as hygiene and security procedures, tools, and protocols, implementing appropriate quality controls and records, in order to ensure good storage conditions (temperatures, humidity and light exposure) and availability of drugs and medical materials according to national regulations
	In collaboration with Medical Coordinator and the HQ Medical Advisor, ensure the implementation of pharmaceutical SOP and tools for stock management, monitoring and evaluation of the pharmacies and health facilities and oversights the adaptation of it to meet the national legislation, in particular for narcotic and psychoactive medicines, ensuring quality and safety of drugs consumption
	Support Medical Coordinator to guarantee the respect of policies for donation of drugs and medical items
	In collaboration with logistics and medical departments and HQ follow up of the medical procurement process (consumables, equipment and medicines), place orders and monitor the purchase in accordance with INTERSOS and donors procedures. Ensure the supply chain is timely, reliable and well documented at all levels
	With the support of HQ Medical Advisor, Medical Coordinator, Pharmacy Manager, Project Managers and logistics department prepare needs assessment and assess local market participating in inspection of local suppliers where appropriate, BoQs and proposal&rsquo;s budget. Ensure the implementation of procedures to obtain import licenses and taxes exemption from the local health and administrative authorities
	Supervise orders of drugs and medical materials in order to ensure an efficient and appropriate consumption and analyze the data to optimize the management of health consumables, equipment and medicines (needs assessment, purchase, control, supply, cold chain, expiry dates, overstock/shortage etc.)
	Participate in base coordination meetings, external meeting, medical team meeting
	Contribute to the writing of donors&rsquo; reports and proposals
	Guarantee data collection and ensure accurate data entry into the pharmacy management software (excel/IMS)
	Support the recruitment process of pharmacy team and strengthen the capacity of pharmacy teams
	Provide training and technical support to the pharmacist manager and staffs in charge of pharmacies on stock management, rational use of medicines, substitution and therapy options and analysis of consumption
	Check, in collaboration with the medical team, the epidemiological data, the status of stocks and consumptions, and assess the appropriateness of prescriptions
	Is responsible to follow up on IMS and to update it in time
	Prepare monthly activity report for the Situation Report
	Ensure the appropriate filing of documentation and archives and share documents with the Medical Coordinator and HQ Medical Unit the relevant data including AMC and stock reports
	Support, in collaboration with Medical Coordinator/TMM/ Logistics Coordinator, the settlement of new warehouse when needed, including management tools and human resources


Required profile and experience


	Pharmaceutical Sciences/Pharmacy Degree
	Minimum three (3) years of working experience in a hospital/health facility set-up or relevant position
	Work experience in medical emergency response projects with NGO desirable
	English
	Essential computer literacy (Word, Advances Excel, Power Point and internet)
	Organizational and communication skills
	Results and quality orientation
	Teamwork and cooperation
	Behavioural flexibility and stress management
	Analytical skills, initiative, independency
	Ability and willingness to travel to remote locations.


General Condition


	Salary:&nbsp;The monthly remuneration is defined, for each position, according to the standard salary grid and function grid, taking into account the experience of the selected candidate. It is communicated during the HR interview, or at any rate during the first stage of the selection process.
	Transportation:&nbsp;Round-trip flights for the humanitarian worker and partner. For any mission lasting at least 12 months, after 9 months, an additional flight ticket to the Country of residence and return to the mission is covered by the Organisation for the humanitarian worker.
	Medical insurance:&nbsp;Injuries and death insurance; civil liability insurance, reimbursement for vaccines, medical certificate and PCR test for deployment; psychosocial support on request for the humanitarian worker.
	Visa:&nbsp;INTERSOS covers the costs and visa support for the humanitarian worker.
	Annual leave:&nbsp;2.5 days per month
	Induction:&nbsp;Online orientation course: each new staff member receives preparatory training before departure, adequate to provide the necessary information related to the structure of INTERSOS and the main decision-making processes and working tools, in order to facilitate entry into the Organisation.

  "description": "Zipline - Do you want to change the world? Zipline uses drones to deliver critical and life-saving medicine to thousands of hospitals serving millions of people in multiple countries. Our mission is to provide every human on Earth with instant access to vital medical supplies. Join Zipline and help us make this a reality for billions of people.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Warehouse Operator

Job Location:&nbsp;Pambegua, Kaduna
Employment type: Fixed-term contract. (2 Years)

About you and the Role&nbsp;&nbsp;


	The&nbsp;Warehouse Operator will be a member of the Fulfillment Operations team, executing several tasks related to but not limited to the preparation of life-saving packages meant for delivery to Zipline&rsquo;s customers.
	These responsibilities encompass picking and packing orders, conducting cycle counts, completing checklists, and ensuring the overall organization and cleanliness of the warehouse.
	This role demands attention to detail, adherence to safety protocols and standard operating procedures, as well as the ability to perform repetitive tasks accurately and efficiently.


Key Responsibilities
Inventory Receipt, Storage, and Audits:


	Accurately receive inventory into the Zipline inventory management software system.
	Utilize inventory management systems to track and record all movements of goods.
	Ensure the received inventory is stored properly within the required storage locations and conditions (temperature requirements).
	Assist in the periodic completion of Stock audits and audit reconciliations.


Picking and Packing:


	Accurately pick products from storage based on incoming orders
	Pack and label picked orders accurately ensuring items are securely packaged while following the standard operating procedures
	Perform quality checks on items picked and packed to ensure compliance with regulatory standards and customer requirements.


General Warehouse Cleanliness and Safety:


	Maintain a clean and organized work environment, adhering to safety and hygiene protocols.
	Complete Accurately, routine checklists for operations.
	Ensure compliance with company policies and safety regulations.


Communication and Collaboration:


	Collaborate closely with specialist ops, flight ops, and customer care teams to coordinate daily operations and prioritize tasks.
	Communicate effectively with team members and supervisors regarding operational issues and opportunities for improvement.
	Support other warehouse functions and team members as required. Participate in continuous improvement initiatives and team meetings.


Required Qualifications


	OND/HND &amp; or Fresh graduate Biology, Chemistry, Environmental Science, Agricultural Science, Microbiology, Medical Lab Tech, Community, Pharmacy Technician, Purchasing and Supply Chain or a related field is a plus.
	Experience: 0-2 years of experience in a warehouse or similar environment preferred.


Key Competencies:


	Strong attention to detail and accuracy in performing repetitive tasks.
	Ability to work efficiently both independently and as part of a team in a fast-paced environment.
	Computer literacy.
	Efficiency in written and spoken communication.
	Proficiency in using inventory management software and handheld devices for order processing.
	Excellent organizational and time management skills.
	Commitment to maintaining a safe working environment and following safety guidelines.
	Reliable and flexible to work occasional overtime and weekends as needed.
	Committed to living the Zipline values: Ownership, Transparency, Customer Obsession, Servant leadership &amp; Continuous improvement.


Working Conditions


	Environment: Warehouse environment with exposure to varying temperatures and noise levels.
	Physical Demands: Requires standing, walking, and lifting products and objects regularly.
	Schedule: Full-time position, may require overtime and weekend work.

Invoicer (Female) Job at  Macden Communications Limited




        
    

        
         Macden Communications Limited - The company is into the sales, supply and distribution of beverages and is a major distributor for Nigerian Breweries Limited, International Breweries PLC, Guiness Nigerian Limited.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Invoicer (Female)

Job Location: Mbaisi, Imo
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilties


	Invoice generation and issuance
	Verification and Reconciliation
	Discrepancy Resolutions
	Customer Service
	Ensuring accuracy in financial records.


Requirements
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The working time and days are from 8am to 6pm and from Mondays to Saturdays and Public Holidays
	Interested applicants must have working experience as an invoicer and must be knowledgeable in business and should be able to use computer softwares.
	Applicants must be resident in Imo state in Mbaise Local Government Area.


Salary
N65,000 - N70,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Note: For more inquiries, candidates should call: 09063461304.
